visa_types.name: D-entry visa
visa_types.documents_required:
When applying for a D-visa, you must fill out a digital application form, gather the documents you need, and go to an application centre to hand in your documents in person.
he documents you are required to hand in with your application depend on the reason you have for applying for an entry visa. You will receive a personal checklist (with an overview of the required documents) once you have completed the digital application form (https://login.udi.no/udiabx.onmicrosoft.com/b2c_1a_signuporsigninidporten_v20/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=adcb8513-e192-444e-b984-2468314ab70b&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fforms.udi.no%2Fauth%2Floginreturn&response_mode=form_post&response_type=code&state=eyJhdXRoTWV0aG9kIjoiZGVmYXVsdCIsIm5vbmNlIjoieXlYcGlkV2UySGQzcHQxaktkMncwIn0%3D&ui_locales=en&scope=openid%20offline_access%20profile).
When you complete the digital application form, you will be notified of the correct application centre for you.
For more information, please visit Entry visas ( D-visa ) - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/entry-visas-d-visas/#link-38884)

visa_types.processing_time:
The Embassy will consider your application for an entry visa. The case processing time in D-visa cases is up to 4 months.
For more information, please visit D-visa - Norway in Jordan (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/)
visa_types.forms: To complete your application form and to also pay your application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Persons who need a visa to visit Norway can in some cases be issued an entry visa, so that they can travel to Norway before they apply or while they are waiting for an answer for their residence permit.
Who can apply for a D-visa?
The D-visa is intended for applicants who:
- Are applying for a residence permit through family immigration, as a student, or as a skilled worker and wish to travel to Norway to submit their application in person to the police.
- Have already applied for a residence permit from abroad (family immigration or study permit) and wish to travel to Norway before receiving a decision on their application.
- Have received a job offer in Norway, and whose employer has obtained confirmation from the police that they may start working before the residence permit application is processed.
- Hold a valid residence permit in Norway but are abroad without their residence card (e.g., lost or stolen).
- Hold a residence permit that has expired while abroad but have applied to renew their permit or obtain permanent residence.
- Will be working at an Embassy or Consulate in Norway, or a family member of a diplomat moving to Norway.
- The new electronic form will be implemented on 13 January 2026. From this date, all applicants must use the D-visa form for new applications, found on the UDI's application portal. The previous practice of using the C-visa form and manually indicating a D-visa, or the printed application form for manual completion, will no longer apply.
Biometric Requirements
In accordance with the Immigration Regulations, biometric data in the form of a facial photograph and fingerprints must be collected and stored for all foreign nationals applying for an entry visa.
Children under six years of age are exempt from fingerprint collection
When an applicant submits an entry visa application together with a residence permit application, it is sufficient to collect the facial photograph and fingerprints only once
You can apply for this kind of visa if you belong to one of the following groups
- spouses of Norwegian citizens who are living in Norway or plan to settle here, and their joint children
- spouses of Nordic nationals who have lived in Norway for the past three years, and their joint children
- spouses of foreign nationals with a permanent residence permit (settlement permit) and their joint children
- spouses of foreign nationals with a residence permit as skilled workers with an employer in Norway and their joint children
- spouses of foreign nationals who have an individual permit to work in a group, and their joint children
Requirements for entry visas
To be granted an entry visa, it must be probable that your application for family immigration will be granted. Among other things, this means that you must meet all the requirements for a family immigration permit and you must have submitted all the documents on the checklist.
How to apply for an entry visa
- Submit an application for a visitor's visa, but write in the application that you wish to be granted an entry visa (D visa).
- If you haven't already submitted your application for family immigration, you must now submit all the documents on the relevant
 
checklist (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/7-day-d-visa/)
 
for family immigration.
- D-visa - Norway in Jordan (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/7-day-d-visa/)
- Have applied: Family immigration - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/have-applied/family-immigration/#link-1228)
- The embassy will consider your application for an entry visa. If your application is rejected, you can submit a written appeal to the embassy. The embassy will consider your application again. If they do not grant it, they will send it to the UDI. The UDI will consider the application again, and either reject it or grant it.
For Re-entry Visas related to expired or lost residence cards, Diplomats (D category) must follow the standard application process. They are required to visit the UDI website, complete the online application form, then visit VFS with the printed application form and the relevant checklist based on the visa type they are applying for.
Kindly find the instructions for D-entry visa for your reference and information Entry Visas ( (D-visas) - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/entry-visas-d-visas/#undefined) )
Note:
VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Persons who need a visa to visit Norway can in some cases be issued an entry visa, so that they can travel to Norway before they apply or while they are waiting for an answer for their residence permit.
Who can apply for a D-visa?
The D-visa is intended for applicants who:
- Are applying for a residence permit through family immigration, as a student, or as a skilled worker and wish to travel to Norway to submit their application in person to the police.
- Have already applied for a residence permit from abroad (family immigration or study permit) and wish to travel to Norway before receiving a decision on their application.
- Have received a job offer in Norway, and whose employer has obtained confirmation from the police that they may start working before the residence permit application is processed.
- Hold a valid residence permit in Norway but are abroad without their residence card (e.g., lost or stolen).
- Hold a residence permit that has expired while abroad but have applied to renew their permit or obtain permanent residence.
- Will be working at an Embassy or Consulate in Norway, or a family member of a diplomat moving to Norway.
- The new electronic form will be implemented on 13 January 2026. From this date, all applicants must use the D-visa form for new applications, found on the UDI's application portal. The previous practice of using the C-visa form and manually indicating a D-visa, or the printed application form for manual completion, will no longer apply.
Biometric Requirements
In accordance with the Immigration Regulations, biometric data in the form of a facial photograph and fingerprints must be collected and stored for all foreign nationals applying for an entry visa.
Children under six years of age are exempt from fingerprint collection
When an applicant submits an entry visa application together with a residence permit application, it is sufficient to collect the facial photograph and fingerprints only once
You can apply for this kind of visa if you belong to one of the following groups
- spouses of Norwegian citizens who are living in Norway or plan to settle here, and their joint children
- spouses of Nordic nationals who have lived in Norway for the past three years, and their joint children
- spouses of foreign nationals with a permanent residence permit (settlement permit) and their joint children
- spouses of foreign nationals with a residence permit as skilled workers with an employer in Norway and their joint children
- spouses of foreign nationals who have an individual permit to work in a group, and their joint children
Requirements for entry visas
To be granted an entry visa, it must be probable that your application for family immigration will be granted. Among other things, this means that you must meet all the requirements for a family immigration permit and you must have submitted all the documents on the checklist.
How to apply for an entry visa
- Submit an application for a visitor's visa, but write in the application that you wish to be granted an entry visa (D visa).
- If you haven't already submitted your application for family immigration, you must now submit all the documents on the relevant
 
checklist (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/7-day-d-visa/)
 
for family immigration.
- D-visa - Norway in Jordan (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/7-day-d-visa/)
- Have applied: Family immigration - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/have-applied/family-immigration/#link-1228)
- The embassy will consider your application for an entry visa. If your application is rejected, you can submit a written appeal to the embassy. The embassy will consider your application again. If they do not grant it, they will send it to the UDI. The UDI will consider the application again, and either reject it or grant it.
For Re-entry Visas related to expired or lost residence cards, Diplomats (D category) must follow the standard application process. They are required to visit the UDI website, complete the online application form, then visit VFS with the printed application form and the relevant checklist based on the visa type they are applying for.
Kindly find the instructions for D-entry visa for your reference and information Entry Visas ( (D-visas) - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/entry-visas-d-visas/#undefined) )
Note:
VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.

## Visa Fees
To check the Visa Fees please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
To complete your application form and to also pay your application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/).

## VFS Global Service Fee:

[Table:Schengen Visa (Type C)]
*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes (VAT 14%) .
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/nor/additional-services)
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
The mode of payment of Service fees is in Cash or with Credit / Debit Card (Only at the Visa Application Centre) the above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at VFS Global visa application center. 
All optional services are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
When applying for a D-visa, you must fill out a digital application form, gather the documents you need, and go to an application centre to hand in your documents in person.
he documents you are required to hand in with your application depend on the reason you have for applying for an entry visa. You will receive a personal checklist (with an overview of the required documents) once you have completed the digital application form (https://login.udi.no/udiabx.onmicrosoft.com/b2c_1a_signuporsigninidporten_v20/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=adcb8513-e192-444e-b984-2468314ab70b&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fforms.udi.no%2Fauth%2Floginreturn&response_mode=form_post&response_type=code&state=eyJhdXRoTWV0aG9kIjoiZGVmYXVsdCIsIm5vbmNlIjoieXlYcGlkV2UySGQzcHQxaktkMncwIn0%3D&ui_locales=en&scope=openid%20offline_access%20profile).
When you complete the digital application form, you will be notified of the correct application centre for you.
For more information, please visit Entry visas ( D-visa ) - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/entry-visas-d-visas/#link-38884)

## Photo Specifications
Photo/passport photo
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised. 
Photo requirements
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
-  the photo must be 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
The Embassy will consider your application for an entry visa. The case processing time in D-visa cases is up to 4 months.
For more information, please visit D-visa - Norway in Jordan (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/services-info/visitors-visa-res-permit/)

## Download Forms
To complete your application form and to also pay your application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/)

visa_types.processing_time: The processing time is 15 days starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application. For processing time at UDI, please check: Waiting time - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/waiting-time/visitor-visa/) Link (https://www.udi.no/en/waiting-time/visitor-visa/)
visa_types.forms: To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country with the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL, please check your travel plans before applying at the Norway Visa Application Centre in Cairo. If you intend to travel to multiple Schengen countries, please apply at the Embassy of the country where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the number of days to be spent in multiple countries is the same, you must apply to the Embassy of the Country which will be your first point of entry.
If you plan to apply for a Schengen visa at the Norway Visa Application Centre, your main destination and the maximum period of stay in the Schengen area must be in Norway. Please provide proof to support this information.
Please note a short-term period visa (type "C") will be for a Maximum length of stay of 90 days only.
For information on General visa requirements and all additional checklists kindly find Short Stay Visa Checklist and please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). 
In case of groups: We strongly suggest that groups contact VFS before they are to submit their applications at VFS’s office in order to simplify the application process. 
Notice: On Monday 13th January 2025 a digital sponsorship and invitation form was made available to individuals, companies, and organisations wishing to sponsor visa applicants to Norway.
The sponsor can now verify their identity by using their Norwegian electronic ID to access ID-porten for individuals or Ansattporten for representatives of companies and organisations. However, to represent a business and act as a sponsor in visa cases, the user needs to have been given permission in Altinn by the business administrator.
The paper version of the sponsorship form will continue to be available alongside the new digital version.
For more information about sponsoring or inviting a visa applicant to Norway visit UDI’s Sponsor for visitors page
According to the Schengen-regulations, the Visa Code article 13, applicants shall give their biometric identifiers when applying for a Schengen-visa.
If the applicant has applied for a Schengen-visa within the last 59 months, the Norwegian Embassy shall copy his biometric identifiers from the common Schengen VIS-database. 
In some cases, the Norwegian Embassy can have problems when copying the biometric identifiers. If the applicant does not manage to copy, the embassy must ask him to come back to VFS to give his biometric identifiers. Therefore, the applicant has the choice if he wants to give his biometric identifiers again or not. 
- Note: VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 01:00 Pm except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the country with the MAIN PURPOSE OF TRAVEL, please check your travel plans before applying at the Norway Visa Application Centre in Cairo. If you intend to travel to multiple Schengen countries, please apply at the Embassy of the country where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the number of days to be spent in multiple countries is the same, you must apply to the Embassy of the Country which will be your first point of entry.
If you plan to apply for a Schengen visa at the Norway Visa Application Centre, your main destination and the maximum period of stay in the Schengen area must be in Norway. Please provide proof to support this information.
Please note a short-term period visa (type "C") will be for a Maximum length of stay of 90 days only.
For information on General visa requirements and all additional checklists kindly find Short Stay Visa Checklist and please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/).
To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). 
In case of groups: We strongly suggest that groups contact VFS before they are to submit their applications at VFS’s office in order to simplify the application process. 
Notice: On Monday 13th January 2025 a digital sponsorship and invitation form was made available to individuals, companies, and organisations wishing to sponsor visa applicants to Norway.
The sponsor can now verify their identity by using their Norwegian electronic ID to access ID-porten for individuals or Ansattporten for representatives of companies and organisations. However, to represent a business and act as a sponsor in visa cases, the user needs to have been given permission in Altinn by the business administrator.
The paper version of the sponsorship form will continue to be available alongside the new digital version.
For more information about sponsoring or inviting a visa applicant to Norway visit UDI’s Sponsor for visitors page
According to the Schengen-regulations, the Visa Code article 13, applicants shall give their biometric identifiers when applying for a Schengen-visa.
If the applicant has applied for a Schengen-visa within the last 59 months, the Norwegian Embassy shall copy his biometric identifiers from the common Schengen VIS-database. 
In some cases, the Norwegian Embassy can have problems when copying the biometric identifiers. If the applicant does not manage to copy, the embassy must ask him to come back to VFS to give his biometric identifiers. Therefore, the applicant has the choice if he wants to give his biometric identifiers again or not. 
- Note: VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 01:00 Pm except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.

## Visa Fees
To check the Visa Fees please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). 

## VFS Global Service Fee:

[Table:Schengen Visa (Type C)]
*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes (VAT 14%) .
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/nor/additional-services)
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
The mode of payment of Service fees is in Cash or with Credit / Debit Card (Only at the Visa Application Centre) the above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at VFS Global visa application center.
All optional services are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

Tourism Short Stay Visa Checklist, please click here.

Visiting family or friends Short Stay Visa Checklist, please click here. 

Business Short Stay Visa Checklist, please click here. 

Cultural or sports events Short Stay Visa Checklist, please click here.

Seafarers Short Stay Visa Checklist, please click here.

Checklist for visa to Persons who are travelling with, or visiting, a family member who is an EU/EEA national please click here. 

Visa Regulation Info - Norway. 

## Photo Specifications

## Photo/passport photo
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised. 
Photo requirements
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
The processing time is 15 days starts from the day the Embassy in Amman receives the application. For processing time at UDI, please check: Waiting time - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/waiting-time/visitor-visa/) Link (https://www.udi.no/en/waiting-time/visitor-visa/)

## Download Forms
 To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are from a country outside the EU/EEA and you want to join a family member in Norway who is an EU/EEA national, you can apply for a residence card for family members of EU/EEA nationals.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Residence Cards for Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/residence-card-for-family-members-of-eueea-nationals-/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
Important Notice:

VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are from a country outside the EU/EEA and you want to join a family member in Norway who is an EU/EEA national, you can apply for a residence card for family members of EU/EEA nationals.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Residence Cards for Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/residence-card-for-family-members-of-eueea-nationals-/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
Important Notice:

VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.

## Visa Fees
To check the Visa Fees please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
Residence Card for Family Member of EU/EEA national has no application fee. You have to fill in the form Application for a permit for residence or work and please contact the Norwegian embassy in Amman by email first (visa.amman@mfa.no), in order to get information about how to proceed.

The mode of payment of Service fees is in Cash or with Credit / Debit Card (Only at the Visa Application Centre) the above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).

## Photo Specifications

## Photo/passport photo
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised. 
Photo requirements
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
For more information about the processing time please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/waiting-time/residency-according-to-the-eueea-and-brexit-regulations/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to 20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays.  
As an au pair, you can improve your language skills and learn about Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian family. In return, you will provide services such as light housework and/or child care for the host family.
Egyptian and Libyan citizens, and persons who have been legally residing in Egypt or Libya the last 6 months, can apply for a residence permit in Norway. You are required to book an appointment in order to submit your application at a Visa application Centre
Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/nor/) for more information. On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) you will find additional information about different residence permits for work, study and other purposes.
Important Notice:
VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to 20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays.  
As an au pair, you can improve your language skills and learn about Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian family. In return, you will provide services such as light housework and/or child care for the host family.
Egyptian and Libyan citizens, and persons who have been legally residing in Egypt or Libya the last 6 months, can apply for a residence permit in Norway. You are required to book an appointment in order to submit your application at a Visa application Centre
Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/nor/) for more information. On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) you will find additional information about different residence permits for work, study and other purposes.
Important Notice:
VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.

## Visa Fees
To check the Visa Fees please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). 

## VFS Global Service Fee:

[Table:Schengen Visa (Type C)]
*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes (VAT 14%) .
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/nor/additional-services)
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
The mode of payment of Service fees is in Cash or with Credit / Debit Card (Only at the Visa Application Centre) the above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at VFS Global visa application center.
All optional services are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
For checklists in residence permit cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)

## Photo Specifications

## Photo/passport photo
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised. 
Photo requirements
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
For more information about the processing time for Study permit please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guide-to-case-processing-times-for-study-permits/).
For more information about the processing time for Work permit please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guide-to-case-processing-times-for-application-for-work-immigration/).

## Download Forms
The application form for a residence permit in Norway needs to be filled online. To register an application online, please log on to the Application Portal Norway (https://login.udi.no/udiabx.onmicrosoft.com/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?p=b2c_1a_signuporsigninidporten&client_id=9bb525d6-40a8-41a1-839c-23a4b7284544&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fmy.udi.no%2Fsignin%2Fb2c_1a_signuporsigninidporten&response_type=id_token&scope=openid%20profile&response_mode=form_post&nonce=638371939284784323.NGYxMzdjZmEtNjEwNi00MDM1LTk3NzYtNjM1NWNkZWZkZmU4ZjY5YmYzZDYtMWU5My00NGZlLThiNjMtZDAxNDhjMzQyNzEy&state=CfDJ8EWQnwbckn9PikQlb7HR6Edexe9kz2DCzfmryR5WhDLhNn_Lz-rODd206RweV6k3OzSdq-8jnWRMZPZlOgvgXyz88sXjtevgzvHujIyAAgFwObARNyvl7ZnI6Jnt5n8Scjht7oCAYAD2L5I73dJBqBmRg3BTJezgvWrF9pBNPfMwCjAO0IxQqj_7xIrexuHqFDHib_qbyaHGiaTLEMXUjmAguCBQk6yVkNXXXb3wkdXOVrbLzyF2IpJMDhD4tYh6J0ab4C3zPNcL1eGOAmWqYqN_sodVoGVNWrWGxpTm1Ue5jVTFIwS-p9OD2tKcZA9iDA&x-client-SKU=ID_NETSTANDARD2_0&x-client-ver=6.10.0.0).
For checklists in residence permit cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).
Form for Granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)

visa_types.name: Residence Permits in Norway
visa_types.overview:
If you intend to stay in Norway for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Norway, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. Please contact Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) for information.
Important Notice:

VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 01:00 Pm except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.
visa_types.full_text:
If you intend to stay in Norway for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Norway, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. Please contact Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) for information.
Important Notice:

VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 01:00 Pm except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.

visa_types.name: Family Immigration
visa_types.documents_required:
You must gather all the documents in the relevant checklist on UDI’s website (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
Please check this list (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf) for information on what documents on the checklist that must be legalized and/or submitted in original. The list also has the names of these documents in Arabic.
Please check the following checklist for family immigration cases

To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/).

visa_types.forms:
The application form for a residence permit in Norway needs to be filled online. To register an application online, please log on to the Application Portal Norway (https://login.udi.no/udiabx.onmicrosoft.com/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?p=b2c_1a_signuporsigninidporten&client_id=9bb525d6-40a8-41a1-839c-23a4b7284544&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fmy.udi.no%2Fsignin%2Fb2c_1a_signuporsigninidporten&response_type=id_token&scope=openid%20profile&response_mode=form_post&nonce=638371983138513360.MWIzMDFhMWMtNDE1NS00ZWU1LTlhZDUtY2UzOWE0ZWQ0YzAzZThkNzMxNTUtN2NkNi00ZDVkLWJhM2YtZTVmNTU0MDc1ODE0&state=CfDJ8EWQnwbckn9PikQlb7HR6EfciwLNP92WNnNtEd1X-drwIscnS4K_GPEylBqAs6N4PaJB625e_FJJh2GigIi6nn3fjQYjuGVztgxsL-jKIgT2sbFpZwW0-L-KIbf1laxHZDzaaNzEEYOgQRwBU07_zWJjZm5C3KkNvYQhZQ8kr_hlxP96_z8rXo6Gg6NKTcIAz55Bb5HEQHTq37k6GqmF09l7n9E1e5-y9-uL8oJFxAMzod7aXWvyTONz8ip0pMtDXEi73VXcMURZvHRSwM5pISNrj1Yr8rz78e6wxt41nspAcMnoGs9VBXfBQxBsoClK_g&x-client-SKU=ID_NETSTANDARD2_0&x-client-ver=6.10.0.0). 
For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/).
Note: For residence permit cases, in case an application appears to be incomplete as per the checklist, the applicant should understand that submitting an incomplete application may lead to the application being rejected. If the applicant insists on submitting an incomplete application, he should sign and fill the following missing documents form declaration. (Documents Declaration Form)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification or forming a family. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings.
Egyptian citizens and other nationals who have been legally residing in Egypt the last 6 months can apply for a family immigration permit in Norway at the Visa Application Center.
On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy  (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) you will find additional information about different family immigration permits and how to apply.
Important Notice:
VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification or forming a family. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings.
Egyptian citizens and other nationals who have been legally residing in Egypt the last 6 months can apply for a family immigration permit in Norway at the Visa Application Center.
On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy  (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) you will find additional information about different family immigration permits and how to apply.
Important Notice:
VFS Global will accept short and long stay applications on walk-in bases in addition to appointments from Sunday till Thursday from 9:00 am till 12:00 PM except for the Wednesdays and public holidays.

## Visa Fees
To check the Visa Fees please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
To complete your visa form and to also pay your visa application fees online click here (https://www.udi.no/en/). 

## VFS Global Service Fee:

[Table:Schengen Visa (Type C)]
*Please note that the service fee is inclusive of all applicable taxes (VAT 14%) .
Click for the price list of additional services (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/egy/en/nor/additional-services)
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
The mode of payment of Service fees is in Cash or with Credit / Debit Card (Only at the Visa Application Centre) the above fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
All Fees are non-refundable once the application has been submitted at VFS Global visa application center.
All optional services are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
You must gather all the documents in the relevant checklist on UDI’s website (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
Please check this list (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf) for information on what documents on the checklist that must be legalized and/or submitted in original. The list also has the names of these documents in Arabic.
Please check the following checklist for family immigration cases

## Photo Specifications

## Photo/passport photo
Everyone who applies for a visa or a residence permit must hand in a photo with their application. The photo must meet certain requirement, so that you are easily recognised. 
Photo requirements
- The photo must show the whole head (the face must take up 70-80 per cent of the photo) and the top of your shoulders.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible with no hair across the eyes.
- Wearing glasses is permitted. The lenses must not be tinted, the frame must not cover parts of your eyes and there must be no reflection in the lenses.
- Religious headgear is permitted if your chin, forehead and both cheeks are clearly visible. The headgear must not cast a shadow on the face.
- the photo must be 
- in sharp focus, clear and with good contrast
- printed on high-quality photo paper at a high resolution
- taken directly from the front, against a light background
- in colour
- 35–40 mm wide
- maximum six months old, and look like you
Please note 
If the photo you enclose does not meet all the requirements, you will be asked to submit a new photo.
If your application also concerns your child, you must submit photos both of you and your child. 
Illustration of photo requirements

## Processing Time
For more information about the processing time please click here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guide-to-case-processing-times-in-family-immigration-cases/)

The application form for a residence permit in Norway needs to be filled online.
